papers.title: HopChain: Multi-Hop Data Synthesis for Generalizable Vision-Language Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract HopChain is a scalable framework that generates multi-hop vision-language reasoning data to enhance VLMs' long-chain reasoning capabilities across diverse benchmarks.  AI-generated summary VLMs show strong multimodal capabilities, but they still struggle with fine-grained vision-language reasoning. We find that long CoT reasoning exposes diverse failure modes, including perception, reasoning, knowledge, and hallucination errors, which can compound across intermediate steps. However, most existing vision-language data used for RLVR does not involve complex reasoning chains that rely on visual evidence throughout, leaving these weaknesses largely unexposed. We therefore propose HopChain, a scalable framework for synthesizing multi-hop vision-language reasoning data specifically for RLVR training of VLMs. Each synthesized multi-hop query forms a logically dependent chain of instance-grounded hops, where earlier hops establish the instances, sets, or conditions needed for later hops, while the final answer remains a specific, unambiguous number suitable for verifiable rewards. We add the multi-hop data synthesized by HopChain to the original RLVR data used to train Qwen3.5-35B-A3B and Qwen3.5-397B-A17B, and compare against RLVR on the original RLVR data alone across 24 benchmarks spanning STEM and Puzzle, General VQA, Text Recognition and Document Understanding, and Video Understanding. Although this multi-hop data is not synthesized to target any specific benchmark, adding it improves 20 out of 24 benchmarks on both models, indicating broad and generalizable gains. To demonstrate that full chained queries are important, we replace them with half-multi-hop or single-hop variants, reducing the 24-benchmark average accuracy by 5.3 and 7.0 points, respectively. Multi-hop training also strengthens long-CoT vision-language reasoning, with gains peaking at more than 50 accuracy points in the ultra-long-CoT regime. These experiments establish HopChain as an effective, scalable framework for synthesizing multi-hop data that improves generalizable vision-language reasoning.

ru.title: Многошаговое рассуждение для более умных мультимодальных моделей
ru.desc: HopChain — это масштабируемый фреймворк для синтеза данных мультишагового рассуждения на основе зрения и языка, предназначенный для улучшения способностей VLM к цепочкам рассуждений. Авторы выявили, что длинные CoT цепочки выявляют разнообразные ошибки в восприятии, рассуждении, знаниях и галлюцинациях, которые накапливаются на промежуточных этапах. В отличие от существующих данных, используемых для RLVR обучения, HopChain создаёт логически зависимые цепочки запросов с несколькими шагами, где каждый шаг опирается на визуальные доказательства предыдущих. Эксперименты показали, что добавление мультишагового обучающего набора улучшает производительность на 20 из 24 бенчмарков и даёт прирост точности более чем на 50 пунктов в режиме длинных цепочек рассуждений.

en.title: Enhancing VLMs with Multi-Hop Reasoning Data
en.desc: HopChain is a new framework designed to create multi-hop vision-language reasoning data, which helps improve the long-chain reasoning abilities of Vision-Language Models (VLMs). The research identifies that existing VLMs struggle with complex reasoning tasks that require multiple steps and visual evidence, leading to various errors. By generating logically dependent chains of reasoning, HopChain allows VLMs to learn from more intricate data, enhancing their performance across a wide range of benchmarks. The results show that incorporating this multi-hop data significantly boosts the accuracy of VLMs in various reasoning tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in training models for better vision-language understanding.

zh.title: HopChain：提升视觉-语言推理的多跳框架
zh.desc: HopChain是一个可扩展的框架，旨在生成多跳视觉-语言推理数据，以增强视觉语言模型（VLMs）在长链推理方面的能力。研究发现，长链推理暴露了多种失败模式，包括感知、推理、知识和幻觉错误，这些错误可能在中间步骤中累积。HopChain通过合成逻辑依赖的多跳查询，提供了一个有效的训练数据集，帮助VLMs在复杂推理链中利用视觉证据。实验表明，使用HopChain合成的数据显著提高了24个基准测试中的20个，证明了其在视觉-语言推理中的广泛适用性。

papers.title: Astrolabe: Steering Forward-Process Reinforcement Learning for Distilled Autoregressive Video Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Astrolabe is an efficient online reinforcement learning framework for distilled autoregressive video models that improves generation quality through forward-process RL formulation and streaming training with multi-reward objectives.  AI-generated summary Distilled autoregressive (AR) video models enable efficient streaming generation but frequently misalign with human visual preferences. Existing reinforcement learning (RL) frameworks are not naturally suited to these architectures, typically requiring either expensive re-distillation or solver-coupled reverse-process optimization that introduces considerable memory and computational overhead. We present Astrolabe, an efficient online RL framework tailored for distilled AR models. To overcome existing bottlenecks, we introduce a forward-process RL formulation based on negative-aware fine-tuning. By contrasting positive and negative samples directly at inference endpoints, this approach establishes an implicit policy improvement direction without requiring reverse-process unrolling. To scale this alignment to long videos, we propose a streaming training scheme that generates sequences progressively via a rolling KV-cache, applying RL updates exclusively to local clip windows while conditioning on prior context to ensure long-range coherence. Finally, to mitigate reward hacking, we integrate a multi-reward objective stabilized by uncertainty-aware selective regularization and dynamic reference updates. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method consistently enhances generation quality across multiple distilled AR video models, serving as a robust and scalable alignment solution.

ru.title: Потоковое обучение с подкреплением для высококачественной генерации видео
ru.desc: Astrolabe — это эффективная система обучения с подкреплением для сжатых авторегрессивных видеомоделей, которая улучшает качество генерации видео. Авторы предлагают формулировку RL, основанную на прямом процессе с отрицательно-осведомлённой настройкой, которая не требует дорогостоящего обратного процесса. Для работы с длинными видео используется потоковая схема обучения с прогрессивной генерацией и локальными обновлениями. Система включает многоцелевую оптимизацию с несколькими наградами и регуляризацией, устойчивой к манипуляциям с функциями вознаграждения.

en.title: Astrolabe: Streamlined Reinforcement Learning for Better Video Generation
en.desc: Astrolabe is a novel online reinforcement learning (RL) framework designed specifically for distilled autoregressive video models. It enhances video generation quality by utilizing a forward-process RL approach that fine-tunes models based on both positive and negative samples. This method avoids the high costs associated with traditional reverse-process optimization, allowing for efficient streaming training of long video sequences. Additionally, Astrolabe incorporates a multi-reward objective to prevent reward hacking, ensuring that the generated content aligns better with human visual preferences.

zh.title: Astrolabe：提升视频生成质量的强化学习新框架
zh.desc: Astrolabe 是一个高效的在线强化学习框架，专为蒸馏自回归视频模型设计。它通过前向过程的强化学习方法和流式训练，结合多重奖励目标，显著提高了生成质量。该框架通过负样本感知微调，直接在推理端对比正负样本，从而实现隐式策略改进。最后，Astrolabe 采用流式训练方案，确保长视频生成的一致性，同时通过不确定性感知的选择性正则化来减轻奖励黑客问题。

papers.title: LumosX: Relate Any Identities with Their Attributes for Personalized Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract LumosX framework enhances text-to-video generation through relational attention mechanisms and structured data pipelines for improved face-attribute alignment and subject consistency.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in diffusion models have significantly improved text-to-video generation, enabling personalized content creation with fine-grained control over both foreground and background elements. However, precise face-attribute alignment across subjects remains challenging, as existing methods lack explicit mechanisms to ensure intra-group consistency. Addressing this gap requires both explicit modeling strategies and face-attribute-aware data resources. We therefore propose LumosX, a framework that advances both data and model design. On the data side, a tailored collection pipeline orchestrates captions and visual cues from independent videos, while multimodal large language models (MLLMs) infer and assign subject-specific dependencies. These extracted relational priors impose a finer-grained structure that amplifies the expressive control of personalized video generation and enables the construction of a comprehensive benchmark. On the modeling side, Relational Self-Attention and Relational Cross-Attention intertwine position-aware embeddings with refined attention dynamics to inscribe explicit subject-attribute dependencies, enforcing disciplined intra-group cohesion and amplifying the separation between distinct subject clusters. Comprehensive evaluations on our benchmark demonstrate that LumosX achieves state-of-the-art performance in fine-grained, identity-consistent, and semantically aligned personalized multi-subject video generation. Code and models are available at https://jiazheng-xing.github.io/lumosx-home/.

ru.title: Реляционное внимание для согласованной многосубъектной генерации видео
ru.desc: LumosX — это фреймворк для генерации видео из текста, который использует механизмы реляционного внимания для улучшения согласованности лиц и субъектов. На уровне данных система применяет специализированный пайплайн с мультимодальными языковыми моделями для извлечения зависимостей между субъектами. На уровне модели вводятся механизмы Relational Self-Attention и Relational Cross-Attention, которые встраивают позиционно-осведомленные эмбеддинги в динамику внимания. Фреймворк достигает передовых результатов в персонализованной мультисубъектной генерации видео с точным выравниванием атрибутов лиц и семантическим соответствием.

en.title: LumosX: Enhancing Text-to-Video Generation with Relational Attention
en.desc: The LumosX framework improves the process of generating videos from text by using advanced relational attention mechanisms and structured data pipelines. It focuses on achieving better alignment of facial attributes and maintaining consistency among subjects in the generated videos. By utilizing multimodal large language models, LumosX enhances the extraction of relational information, which helps in creating more personalized and coherent video content. The framework has been evaluated against a comprehensive benchmark, showing superior performance in generating identity-consistent and semantically aligned videos.

zh.title: LumosX：提升文本到视频生成的框架
zh.desc: LumosX框架通过关系注意机制和结构化数据管道，增强了文本到视频的生成效果，特别是在面部属性对齐和主体一致性方面。该框架利用多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）提取和分配主体特定的依赖关系，从而提高个性化视频生成的控制能力。LumosX在数据和模型设计上都有所创新，确保了组内一致性和不同主体之间的分离。综合评估表明，LumosX在细粒度、一致性和语义对齐的多主体视频生成方面达到了最先进的性能。

papers.title: A Subgoal-driven Framework for Improving Long-Horizon LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract LLM-based agents for web navigation face challenges in long-horizon planning and reinforcement learning fine-tuning, which are addressed through subgoal decomposition and milestone-based reward signals, significantly improving success rates over existing proprietary and open models.  AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM)-based agents have emerged as powerful autonomous controllers for digital environments, including mobile interfaces, operating systems, and web browsers. Web navigation, for example, requires handling dynamic content and long sequences of actions, making it particularly challenging. Existing LLM-based agents struggle with long-horizon planning in two main ways. During online execution, they often lose track as new information arrives, lacking a clear and adaptive path toward the final goal. This issue is further exacerbated during reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning, where sparse and delayed rewards make it difficult for agents to identify which actions lead to success, preventing them from maintaining coherent reasoning over extended tasks. To address these challenges, we propose two contributions. First, we introduce an agent framework that leverages proprietary models for online planning through subgoal decomposition. Second, we present MiRA (Milestoning your Reinforcement Learning Enhanced Agent), an RL training framework that uses dense, milestone-based reward signals. The real-time planning mechanism improves proprietary models such as Gemini by approximately a 10% absolute increase in success rate (SR) on the WebArena-Lite benchmark. Meanwhile, applying MiRA to the open Gemma3-12B model increases its success rate from 6.4% to 43.0%. This performance surpasses proprietary systems such as GPT-4-Turbo (17.6%) and GPT-4o (13.9%), as well as the previous open-model state of the art, WebRL (38.4%). Overall, our findings demonstrate that combining explicit inference-time planning with milestone-based rewards significantly improves an agent's long-horizon capabilities, paving the way for more robust and general-purpose autonomous systems.

ru.title: Навигация по веб-сайтам через декомпозицию цели и премиум-вознаграждения
ru.desc: В работе предложен подход для улучшения способностей LLM-агентов к долгосрочному планированию при навигации по веб-сайтам. Авторы используют декомпозицию цели на подцели для планирования в реальном времени и представляют метод MiRA, который применяет плотные вознаграждения на основе промежуточных этапов при обучении с подкреплением. Эти методы решают проблему разреженных отложенных наград, которые затрудняют агентам понимание последовательностей действий, ведущих к успеху. Результаты показывают значительный прирост эффективности: модель Gemma3-12B улучшила результат с 6,4% до 43,0%, превзойдя закрытые модели GPT-4 и предыдущие открытые системы.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Agents with Subgoals and Milestones for Better Web Navigation
en.desc: This paper discusses the challenges faced by large language model (LLM)-based agents in web navigation, particularly in long-horizon planning and reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning. The authors propose a new framework that uses subgoal decomposition to enhance online planning, allowing agents to maintain a clearer path towards their goals despite dynamic content. Additionally, they introduce MiRA, a training method that employs milestone-based reward signals to provide more immediate feedback during RL, significantly improving the agents' performance. The results show that these innovations lead to substantial increases in success rates, outperforming both proprietary and existing open models.

zh.title: 提升网络导航能力的智能代理新方法
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了基于大型语言模型（LLM）的代理在网络导航中的挑战，特别是在长时间规划和强化学习微调方面。为了解决这些问题，提出了子目标分解和基于里程碑的奖励信号的方法，这显著提高了成功率。通过实时规划机制，改进了现有的专有模型，并在WebArena-Lite基准测试中取得了约10%的成功率提升。同时，应用MiRA框架使开放模型Gemma3-12B的成功率从6.4%提升至43.0%，超越了许多现有的专有系统。

papers.title: How Well Does Generative Recommendation Generalize?
papers.abstract: Abstract Generative recommendation models excel at generalization tasks while item ID-based models perform better at memorization, with a complementary approach showing improved recommendation performance through adaptive combination.  AI-generated summary A widely held hypothesis for why generative recommendation (GR) models outperform conventional item ID-based models is that they generalize better. However, there is few systematic way to verify this hypothesis beyond a superficial comparison of overall performance. To address this gap, we categorize each data instance based on the specific capability required for a correct prediction: either memorization (reusing item transition patterns observed during training) or generalization (composing known patterns to predict unseen item transitions). Extensive experiments show that GR models perform better on instances that require generalization, whereas item ID-based models perform better when memorization is more important. To explain this divergence, we shift the analysis from the item level to the token level and show that what appears to be item-level generalization often reduces to token-level memorization for GR models. Finally, we show that the two paradigms are complementary. We propose a simple memorization-aware indicator that adaptively combines them on a per-instance basis, leading to improved overall recommendation performance.

ru.title: Синтез запоминания и обобщения в рекомендательных системах
ru.desc: В статье исследуется компромисс между генеративными моделями рекомендаций, которые лучше обобщают на новые данные, и моделями на основе ID товаров, которые лучше запоминают паттерны из обучающего набора. Авторы разработали методику категоризации примеров в данных по требуемой способности: либо запоминание известных переходов между товарами, либо обобщение для предсказания невидимых переходов. Анализ на уровне токенов показал, что кажущееся обобщение на уровне товаров часто сводится к запоминанию на уровне токенов в генеративных моделях. Предложен адаптивный метод комбинирования обоих подходов на основе индикатора, специфичного для каждого примера, что улучшает общее качество рекомендаций.

en.title: Combining Strengths: Enhancing Recommendations through Adaptive Memorization and Generalization
en.desc: This paper investigates the performance differences between generative recommendation (GR) models and item ID-based models in recommendation systems. It categorizes data instances based on whether they require memorization or generalization for accurate predictions. The findings reveal that GR models excel in generalization tasks, while item ID-based models are superior in memorization tasks. The authors propose a method to combine both approaches adaptively, enhancing overall recommendation performance by leveraging the strengths of each model type.

zh.title: 生成与记忆的完美结合，提升推荐效果
zh.desc: 生成推荐模型在泛化任务上表现优异，而基于项目ID的模型在记忆任务上更具优势。本文通过对数据实例进行分类，探讨了记忆和泛化在推荐中的作用。实验结果表明，生成推荐模型在需要泛化的情况下表现更好，而基于项目ID的模型在记忆更重要时表现更佳。我们提出了一种简单的记忆感知指标，能够根据实例自适应地结合这两种模型，从而提高整体推荐性能。

papers.title: BEAVER: A Training-Free Hierarchical Prompt Compression Method via Structure-Aware Page Selection
papers.abstract: Abstract BEAVER is a training-free framework that improves long-context LLM inference by using structure-aware hierarchical selection and dense tensor mapping to reduce latency while maintaining semantic integrity.  AI-generated summary The exponential expansion of context windows in LLMs has unlocked capabilities for long-document understanding but introduced severe bottlenecks in inference latency and information utilization. Existing compression methods often suffer from high training costs or semantic fragmentation due to aggressive token pruning. In this paper, we propose BEAVER, a novel training-free framework that shifts compression from linear token removal to structure-aware hierarchical selection. BEAVER maximizes hardware parallelism by mapping variable-length contexts into dense page-level tensors via dual-path pooling, and preserves discourse integrity through a hybrid planner combining semantic and lexical dual-branch selection with sentence smoothing. Extensive evaluations on four long-context benchmarks demonstrate that BEAVER achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods like LongLLMLingua. Notably, on the RULER benchmark, BEAVER maintains high fidelity in multi-needle retrieval where baselines deteriorate. Regarding efficiency, BEAVER reduces latency by 26.4x on 128k contexts, offering a scalable solution for high-throughput applications. Our code is available at https://cslikai.cn/BEAVER/.
affiliations: Ant Group Security and Intelligence Laboratory (SIL)
School of Computer Technology and Application, Qinghai University

ru.title: Структурно-осознанное сжатие контекста без обучения для ускорения вывода LLM
ru.desc: BEAVER — это фреймворк для сжатия контекста больших языковых моделей, который работает без дополнительного обучения. Метод использует иерархическую селекцию токенов с учётом структуры текста и преобразует переменной длины контексты в плотные тензоры, сохраняя при этом семантическую целостность. Гибридный планировщик комбинирует семантический и лексический анализ с целью избежать фрагментации дискурса, что превосходит агрессивное усечение токенов. На практике BEAVER достигает ускорения в 26.4 раза при обработке длинных контекстов (128k токенов) и демонстрирует конкурентные результаты на бенчмарках долгоконтекстного понимания.

en.title: BEAVER: Fast and Smart Inference for Long-Context LLMs
en.desc: BEAVER is a novel framework designed to enhance the inference speed of long-context large language models (LLMs) without the need for training. It employs a structure-aware hierarchical selection method to efficiently compress information, avoiding the pitfalls of traditional token pruning that can lead to loss of meaning. By utilizing dense tensor mapping and dual-path pooling, BEAVER optimizes hardware usage and maintains the integrity of the discourse. Evaluations show that BEAVER not only matches the performance of leading methods but also significantly reduces latency, making it a promising solution for applications requiring rapid processing of extensive text.

zh.title: BEAVER：提升长上下文推理效率的无训练框架
zh.desc: BEAVER是一个无训练的框架，旨在通过结构感知的层次选择和密集张量映射来提高长上下文大语言模型（LLM）的推理效率。该方法通过将压缩从线性去除标记转变为层次选择，最大化硬件并行性，并通过双路径池化将可变长度的上下文映射为密集的页面级张量。BEAVER在保持语义完整性的同时，结合语义和词汇的双分支选择与句子平滑，确保了话语的一致性。经过在四个长上下文基准上的广泛评估，BEAVER在性能上与最先进的方法相当，同时在效率上显著降低了推理延迟。

papers.title: WorldAgents: Can Foundation Image Models be Agents for 3D World Models?
papers.abstract: Abstract 2D image models possess inherent 3D world modeling capabilities that can be harnessed through an agentic framework for 3D scene synthesis and reconstruction.  AI-generated summary Given the remarkable ability of 2D foundation image models to generate high-fidelity outputs, we investigate a fundamental question: do 2D foundation image models inherently possess 3D world model capabilities? To answer this, we systematically evaluate multiple state-of-the-art image generation models and Vision-Language Models (VLMs) on the task of 3D world synthesis. To harness and benchmark their potential implicit 3D capability, we propose an agentic framing to facilitate 3D world generation. Our approach employs a multi-agent architecture: a VLM-based director that formulates prompts to guide image synthesis, a generator that synthesizes new image views, and a VLM-backed two-step verifier that evaluates and selectively curates generated frames from both 2D image and 3D reconstruction space. Crucially, we demonstrate that our agentic approach provides coherent and robust 3D reconstruction, producing output scenes that can be explored by rendering novel views. Through extensive experiments across various foundation models, we demonstrate that 2D models do indeed encapsulate a grasp of 3D worlds. By exploiting this understanding, our method successfully synthesizes expansive, realistic, and 3D-consistent worlds.

ru.title: Раскрытие трёхмерного мира в двумерных моделях через мультиагентный синтез
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что двумерные foundation models для генерации изображений содержат скрытые способности к моделированию трёхмерного мира. Авторы предлагают мультиагентный фреймворк, где Vision-Language Model выступает режиссёром, генератор синтезирует новые виды сцены, а верификатор оценивает качество генерируемых кадров в 2D и 3D пространстве. Метод обеспечивает согласованную 3D реконструкцию, позволяя исследовать синтезированные сцены с помощью рендеринга новых ракурсов. Результаты демонстрируют, что современные 2D модели действительно обладают имплицитным пониманием трёхмерной геометрии и могут успешно генерировать объёмные, реалистичные миры.

en.title: Unlocking 3D Worlds from 2D Image Models
en.desc: This paper explores the ability of 2D image models to understand and generate 3D scenes. It introduces an agentic framework that utilizes a multi-agent system, including a Vision-Language Model (VLM) director, a generator for creating new image views, and a verifier for evaluating the outputs. The research shows that these 2D models can implicitly capture 3D world structures, allowing for coherent and realistic scene synthesis. By leveraging this capability, the proposed method can produce expansive and consistent 3D environments that can be navigated through novel view rendering.

zh.title: 二维模型的三维世界潜力
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了二维图像模型是否具备内在的三维世界建模能力。我们通过评估多种先进的图像生成模型和视觉语言模型，系统地研究了三维世界合成的任务。为此，我们提出了一种代理框架，利用多代理架构来促进三维世界的生成。实验结果表明，二维模型确实能够理解三维世界，从而成功合成出广阔、真实且一致的三维场景。

papers.title: Beyond Single Tokens: Distilling Discrete Diffusion Models via Discrete MMD
papers.abstract: Abstract Discrete Moment Matching Distillation (D-MMD) enables effective distillation of discrete diffusion models by adapting continuous-domain techniques, achieving superior performance compared to previous methods.  AI-generated summary It is currently difficult to distill discrete diffusion models. In contrast, continuous diffusion literature has many distillation approaches methods that can reduce sampling steps to a handful.   Our method, Discrete Moment Matching Distillation (D-MMD), leverages ideas that have been highly successful in the continuous domain. Whereas previous discrete distillation methods collapse, D-MMD maintains high quality and diversity (given sufficient sampling steps). This is demonstrated on both text and image datasets. Moreover, the newly distilled generators can outperform their teachers.

ru.title: Перенос знаний из непрерывности в дискретность: дистилляция диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод Discrete Moment Matching Distillation (D-MMD) для дистилляции дискретных диффузионных моделей, который адаптирует успешные техники из непрерывной области. Метод решает проблему коллапса качества, который наблюдается в предыдущих подходах к дистилляции дискретных моделей, сохраняя высокое качество и разнообразие выходов. D-MMD демонстрирует эффективность на текстовых и графических датасетах, позволяя дистиллированным моделям превосходить свои учительские модели. Этот подход значительно сокращает количество необходимых шагов выборки при сохранении производительности.

en.title: Elevating Discrete Diffusion: D-MMD for Superior Model Distillation
en.desc: Discrete Moment Matching Distillation (D-MMD) is a novel approach for distilling discrete diffusion models by utilizing techniques from the continuous domain. This method addresses the challenges faced in distilling discrete models, which often suffer from quality loss. D-MMD effectively reduces the number of sampling steps while preserving high quality and diversity in generated outputs. The results show that the distilled models not only maintain performance but can also surpass the original models in both text and image generation tasks.

zh.title: 离散扩散模型的高效蒸馏新方法
zh.desc: 离散时刻匹配蒸馏（D-MMD）是一种有效的蒸馏离散扩散模型的方法，它通过适应连续领域的技术来实现。与之前的方法相比，D-MMD在性能上表现更优。该方法利用了在连续领域中非常成功的思想，能够保持高质量和多样性，尤其是在足够的采样步骤下。实验结果表明，新蒸馏的生成器在文本和图像数据集上能够超越其教师模型。

papers.title: Hyperagents
papers.abstract: Abstract Hyperagents represent a self-referential framework that integrates task and meta-agents into a single editable program, enabling metacognitive self-modification and open-ended improvement across diverse computational domains.  AI-generated summary Self-improving AI systems aim to reduce reliance on human engineering by learning to improve their own learning and problem-solving processes. Existing approaches to self-improvement rely on fixed, handcrafted meta-level mechanisms, fundamentally limiting how fast such systems can improve. The Darwin Gödel Machine (DGM) demonstrates open-ended self-improvement in coding by repeatedly generating and evaluating self-modified variants. Because both evaluation and self-modification are coding tasks, gains in coding ability can translate into gains in self-improvement ability. However, this alignment does not generally hold beyond coding domains. We introduce hyperagents, self-referential agents that integrate a task agent (which solves the target task) and a meta agent (which modifies itself and the task agent) into a single editable program. Crucially, the meta-level modification procedure is itself editable, enabling metacognitive self-modification, improving not only the task-solving behavior, but also the mechanism that generates future improvements. We instantiate this framework by extending DGM to create DGM-Hyperagents (DGM-H), eliminating the assumption of domain-specific alignment between task performance and self-modification skill to potentially support self-accelerating progress on any computable task. Across diverse domains, the DGM-H improves performance over time and outperforms baselines without self-improvement or open-ended exploration, as well as prior self-improving systems. Furthermore, the DGM-H improves the process by which it generates new agents (e.g., persistent memory, performance tracking), and these meta-level improvements transfer across domains and accumulate across runs. DGM-Hyperagents offer a glimpse of open-ended AI systems that do not merely search for better solutions, but continually improve their search for how to improve.

ru.title: Самоулучшающиеся системы, которые совершенствуют способ своего совершенствования
ru.desc: В этой работе представлены гиперагенты — самоссылающаяся архитектура, которая объединяет задачный агент и мета-агент в одну редактируемую программу. Ключевая инновация заключается в том, что сама процедура модификации является редактируемой, позволяя агентам улучшать не только решение основной задачи, но и механизм собственного совершенствования. Авторы расширяют Darwin Gödel Machine в виде DGM-Hyperagents, избегая предположения о выравнивании между производительностью и способностью к самомодификации, что позволяет применять подход к разнообразным вычислительным задачам. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что система непрерывно улучшает как качество решений, так и процесс генерации новых версий себя, с накоплением улучшений на протяжении времени.

en.title: Hyperagents: AI That Self-Improves and Evolves!
en.desc: Hyperagents are a new type of AI framework that combines two types of agents: a task agent that focuses on solving specific problems and a meta agent that modifies itself and the task agent. This integration allows the AI to not only improve its problem-solving abilities but also enhance the methods it uses to make those improvements. The DGM-Hyperagents extend the capabilities of previous self-improving systems by allowing for continuous, domain-independent enhancements, leading to better performance over time. This approach represents a significant step towards creating AI that can autonomously evolve and optimize its own learning processes.

zh.title: 超代理：自我改进的智能系统新视野
zh.desc: 超代理（Hyperagents）是一种自我参考的框架，将任务代理和元代理整合为一个可编辑的程序，能够实现元认知自我修改和开放式改进。现有的自我改进方法依赖于固定的手工设计的元级机制，限制了系统的改进速度。达尔文哥德尔机器（DGM）展示了在编码领域的开放式自我改进，通过反复生成和评估自我修改的变体来实现。我们提出的超代理消除了任务表现与自我修改技能之间的特定领域对齐假设，支持在任何可计算任务上的自我加速进展。

papers.title: EgoForge: Goal-Directed Egocentric World Simulator
papers.abstract: Abstract EgoForge is an egocentric goal-directed world simulator that generates coherent first-person video rollouts from minimal static inputs using trajectory-level reward-guided refinement during diffusion sampling.  AI-generated summary Generative world models have shown promise for simulating dynamic environments, yet egocentric video remains challenging due to rapid viewpoint changes, frequent hand-object interactions, and goal-directed procedures whose evolution depends on latent human intent. Existing approaches either focus on hand-centric instructional synthesis with limited scene evolution, perform static view translation without modeling action dynamics, or rely on dense supervision, such as camera trajectories, long video prefixes, synchronized multicamera capture, etc. In this work, we introduce EgoForge, an egocentric goal-directed world simulator that generates coherent, first-person video rollouts from minimal static inputs: a single egocentric image, a high-level instruction, and an optional auxiliary exocentric view. To improve intent alignment and temporal consistency, we propose VideoDiffusionNFT, a trajectory-level reward-guided refinement that optimizes goal completion, temporal causality, scene consistency, and perceptual fidelity during diffusion sampling. Extensive experiments show EgoForge achieves consistent gains in semantic alignment, geometric stability, and motion fidelity over strong baselines, and robust performance in real-world smart-glasses experiments.

ru.title: Генерация эгоцентрического видео с помощью вознаграждаемой диффузии
ru.desc: EgoForge — это генеративная мировая модель, которая синтезирует видео от первого лица на основе минимальных входных данных: одного кадра, текстового описания задачи и опционального внешнего вида. Модель использует диффузионный процесс с направлением на уровне траектории, где функция вознаграждения оптимизирует завершение цели, причинно-следственные связи и консистентность сцены. Подход решает сложные проблемы синтеза эгоцентрического видео, включая быстрые изменения точки зрения, взаимодействия рук с объектами и процедуры, зависящие от скрытого намерения пользователя. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют улучшение в семантическом выравнивании, геометрической стабильности и качестве движения по сравнению с базовыми моделями.

en.title: EgoForge: Revolutionizing First-Person Video Generation
en.desc: EgoForge is a novel simulator designed to create realistic first-person videos by using minimal input data, such as a single image and a high-level instruction. It addresses the challenges of generating egocentric videos, which often involve complex interactions and rapid viewpoint changes. The key innovation is the VideoDiffusionNFT method, which refines video outputs by focusing on achieving specific goals and maintaining temporal consistency. Experimental results demonstrate that EgoForge outperforms existing methods in terms of semantic alignment and motion fidelity, making it effective for real-world applications like smart glasses.

zh.title: EgoForge：自我导向的世界模拟新突破
zh.desc: EgoForge 是一个以自我为中心的目标导向世界模拟器，能够从最少的静态输入生成连贯的第一人称视频。它使用轨迹级奖励引导的细化方法，在扩散采样过程中优化目标完成、时间因果关系、场景一致性和感知保真度。与现有方法相比，EgoForge 在语义对齐、几何稳定性和运动保真度方面表现出显著优势。通过大量实验，EgoForge 在真实世界的智能眼镜实验中也展现了强大的性能。

papers.title: FlowScene: Style-Consistent Indoor Scene Generation with Multimodal Graph Rectified Flow
papers.abstract: Abstract FlowScene is a tri-branch generative model that combines multimodal graph conditioning with rectified flow modeling to produce realistic scenes with controlled geometry, appearance, and stylistic coherence.  AI-generated summary Scene generation has extensive industrial applications, demanding both high realism and precise control over geometry and appearance. Language-driven retrieval methods compose plausible scenes from a large object database, but overlook object-level control and often fail to enforce scene-level style coherence. Graph-based formulations offer higher controllability over objects and inform holistic consistency by explicitly modeling relations, yet existing methods struggle to produce high-fidelity textured results, thereby limiting their practical utility. We present FlowScene, a tri-branch scene generative model conditioned on multimodal graphs that collaboratively generates scene layouts, object shapes, and object textures. At its core lies a tight-coupled rectified flow model that exchanges object information during generation, enabling collaborative reasoning across the graph. This enables fine-grained control of objects' shapes, textures, and relations while enforcing scene-level style coherence across structure and appearance. Extensive experiments show that FlowScene outperforms both language-conditioned and graph-conditioned baselines in terms of generation realism, style consistency, and alignment with human preferences.
affiliations: Beijing Digital Native Digital City Research Center
School of Computer Science, Peking University

ru.title: Граф управляет творчеством: стильная генерация сцен через выпрямленные потоки
ru.desc: FlowScene — это генеративная модель с тремя ветвями, которая использует мультимодальные графы и выпрямленные потоки для создания реалистичных сцен. Модель позволяет одновременно контролировать геометрию объектов, их внешний вид и стилистическую согласованность всей сцены. Ключевая особенность заключается в плотно связанной архитектуре потока, которая обменивается информацией об объектах во время генерации, обеспечивая совместное рассуждение. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство FlowScene над методами, основанными на языке и графах, по реализму, консистентности стиля и соответствию предпочтениям человека.

en.title: FlowScene: Mastering Scene Generation with Precision and Style
en.desc: FlowScene is a novel tri-branch generative model designed for creating realistic scenes with precise control over geometry and appearance. It utilizes multimodal graph conditioning and a rectified flow model to enhance the generation process by allowing collaborative reasoning among objects. This approach improves the control over individual object shapes and textures while maintaining overall style coherence in the generated scenes. Experimental results demonstrate that FlowScene surpasses existing methods in realism, style consistency, and alignment with human preferences.

zh.title: FlowScene：生成真实场景的智能模型
zh.desc: FlowScene是一种三分支生成模型，结合了多模态图条件和修正流建模，能够生成具有控制几何形状、外观和风格一致性的真实场景。该模型通过图形化的方式提供了更高的对象控制能力，并显式建模对象之间的关系，从而实现整体一致性。FlowScene的核心是一个紧密耦合的修正流模型，在生成过程中交换对象信息，促进图形之间的协同推理。实验结果表明，FlowScene在生成真实感、风格一致性和与人类偏好的对齐方面，优于现有的语言条件和图条件基线。

papers.title: AgentDS Technical Report: Benchmarking the Future of Human-AI Collaboration in Domain-Specific Data Science
papers.abstract: Abstract AgentDS benchmark evaluates AI agents and human-AI collaboration in domain-specific data science tasks, revealing continued necessity of human expertise despite advances in large language models and AI agents.  AI-generated summary Data science plays a critical role in transforming complex data into actionable insights across numerous domains. Recent developments in large language models (LLMs) and artificial intelligence (AI) agents have significantly automated data science workflow. However, it remains unclear to what extent AI agents can match the performance of human experts on domain-specific data science tasks, and in which aspects human expertise continues to provide advantages. We introduce AgentDS, a benchmark and competition designed to evaluate both AI agents and human-AI collaboration performance in domain-specific data science. AgentDS consists of 17 challenges across six industries: commerce, food production, healthcare, insurance, manufacturing, and retail banking. We conducted an open competition involving 29 teams and 80 participants, enabling systematic comparison between human-AI collaborative approaches and AI-only baselines. Our results show that current AI agents struggle with domain-specific reasoning. AI-only baselines perform near or below the median of competition participants, while the strongest solutions arise from human-AI collaboration. These findings challenge the narrative of complete automation by AI and underscore the enduring importance of human expertise in data science, while illuminating directions for the next generation of AI. Visit the AgentDS website here: https://agentds.org/ and open source datasets here: https://huggingface.co/datasets/lainmn/AgentDS .
affiliations: Carlson School of Management, University of Minnesota
Data Science Institute, University of Chicago
Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of Minnesota
Division of Computational Health Sciences, University of Minnesota
School of Statistics, University of Minnesota

ru.title: AI нужна помощь человека: вместе сильнее
ru.desc: Представлена бенчмарк AgentDS для оценки способности AI-агентов и человеко-машинного сотрудничества решать специализированные задачи в области науки о данных на примере 17 задач из шести различных отраслей. Исследование включило открытое соревнование с участием 29 команд и 80 участников, которые сравнивали подходы человеко-машинного сотрудничества с полностью автоматизированными решениями на основе нейросетей. Результаты показали, что современные AI-агенты испытывают значительные трудности с доменоспецифичным рассуждением и отстают по производительности от средних результатов участников конкурса. Наиболее сильные решения получены при гибридном подходе, объединяющем возможности AI и человеческого опыта, что подтверждает неизбежность человеческого участия в науке о данных.

en.title: Human Expertise: Essential in Data Science Despite AI Advances
en.desc: The AgentDS benchmark evaluates the performance of AI agents and their collaboration with humans in specific data science tasks across various industries. Despite advancements in large language models and AI agents, the study reveals that human expertise remains crucial for effective domain-specific reasoning. The competition involved 29 teams and highlighted that AI-only solutions often performed poorly compared to human-AI collaborative efforts. These results emphasize the limitations of current AI technologies and the ongoing need for human insight in data science workflows.

zh.title: 人类智慧与AI协作，数据科学的未来之路
zh.desc: AgentDS基准测试评估了人工智能代理和人类与人工智能的协作在特定领域数据科学任务中的表现。尽管大型语言模型和人工智能代理的进步显著自动化了数据科学工作流程，但人类专家的专业知识仍然至关重要。研究表明，当前的人工智能代理在特定领域推理方面存在困难，且仅依靠人工智能的基线表现往往低于竞争参与者的中位数。最强的解决方案来自于人类与人工智能的协作，这突显了人类专业知识在数据科学中的持久重要性。

papers.title: Language on Demand, Knowledge at Core: Composing LLMs with Encoder-Decoder Translation Models for Extensible Multilinguality
papers.abstract: Abstract XBridge is a compositional architecture that combines pretrained translation models with large language models to improve multilingual performance while maintaining the LLM's general knowledge processing capabilities.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong general intelligence, yet their multilingual performance remains highly imbalanced. Although LLMs encode substantial cross-lingual knowledge in a unified semantic space, they often struggle to reliably interface this knowledge with low-resource or unseen languages. Fortunately, pretrained encoder-decoder translation models already possess balanced multilingual capability, suggesting a natural complement to LLMs. In this work, we propose XBridge, a compositional encoder-LLM-decoder architecture that offloads multilingual understanding and generation to external pretrained translation models, while preserving the LLM as an English-centric core for general knowledge processing. To address the resulting representation misalignment across models, we introduce lightweight cross-model mapping layers and an optimal transport-based alignment objective, enabling fine-grained semantic consistency for multilingual generation. Experiments on four LLMs across multilingual understanding, reasoning, summarization, and generation indicate that XBridge outperforms strong baselines, especially on low-resource and previously unseen languages, without retraining the LLM.
affiliations: Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences
State Key Laboratory of AI Safety, Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Мост между языками: комбинирование переводчиков и больших моделей
ru.desc: XBridge — это композиционная архитектура, которая объединяет предобученные модели машинного перевода с большими языковыми моделями для улучшения многоязычной производительности. Система использует лёгкие слои отображения между моделями и оптимальный транспорт для достижения семантической согласованности при переводе. Архитектура делегирует многоязычное понимание и генерацию внешним моделям перевода, сохраняя LLM как ядро для обработки общих знаний на английском языке. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что XBridge превосходит существующие решения на низкоресурсных и ранее неизвестных языках без переобучения исходной модели.

en.title: Bridging Multilingual Gaps with XBridge
en.desc: XBridge is a new architecture that combines pretrained translation models with large language models (LLMs) to enhance their ability to work with multiple languages. While LLMs are good at general knowledge, they often struggle with languages that are less common or not seen before. XBridge uses translation models to handle multilingual tasks, allowing the LLM to focus on its strengths in English and general knowledge. The architecture includes special layers to align the different models, ensuring they work well together and improve performance in multilingual tasks without needing to retrain the LLM.

zh.title: XBridge：提升多语言能力的创新架构
zh.desc: XBridge是一种组合架构，它将预训练的翻译模型与大型语言模型（LLM）结合，以提高多语言性能，同时保持LLM的通用知识处理能力。尽管LLM在一般智能方面表现出色，但其多语言性能仍然不平衡。XBridge通过将多语言理解和生成任务转移到外部的预训练翻译模型上，解决了LLM在低资源或未见语言上的表现问题。实验表明，XBridge在多语言理解、推理、摘要和生成任务上优于强基线，尤其是在低资源和未见语言上，且无需重新训练LLM。

papers.title: Teaching an Agent to Sketch One Part at a Time
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-modal language model-based agent generates vector sketches incrementally using part-level annotations and process-reward reinforcement learning with visual feedback.  AI-generated summary We develop a method for producing vector sketches one part at a time. To do this, we train a multi-modal language model-based agent using a novel multi-turn process-reward reinforcement learning following supervised fine-tuning. Our approach is enabled by a new dataset we call ControlSketch-Part, containing rich part-level annotations for sketches, obtained using a novel, generic automatic annotation pipeline that segments vector sketches into semantic parts and assigns paths to parts with a structured multi-stage labeling process. Our results indicate that incorporating structured part-level data and providing agent with the visual feedback through the process enables interpretable, controllable, and locally editable text-to-vector sketch generation.

ru.title: Пошаговая генерация эскизов с обучением на процессе и визуальной обратной связью
ru.desc: В работе представлен метод генерации векторных эскизов пошагово с использованием многомодальной языковой модели. Авторы применили обучение с подкреплением на основе reward-моделей процесса с визуальной обратной связью после поэтапной тонкой настройки модели. Для обучения создан новый датасет ControlSketch-Part с детальной разметкой на уровне отдельных частей эскизов, полученной через автоматический конвейер сегментации. Предложенный подход обеспечивает интерпретируемую, управляемую и локально редактируемую генерацию векторных эскизов из текстовых описаний.

en.title: Incremental Vector Sketch Generation with Multi-Modal Learning
en.desc: This paper presents a method for generating vector sketches incrementally, focusing on creating one part at a time. It utilizes a multi-modal language model-based agent that learns through a process-reward reinforcement learning framework, enhanced by visual feedback. The training is supported by a new dataset called ControlSketch-Part, which includes detailed part-level annotations for sketches. The findings show that using structured data and visual feedback allows for more interpretable and controllable generation of sketches from text descriptions.

zh.title: 逐步生成可控的矢量草图
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种基于多模态语言模型的代理，能够逐步生成矢量草图。我们使用一种新颖的多轮过程奖励强化学习方法，结合监督微调来训练该代理。研究中使用了一个名为ControlSketch-Part的新数据集，该数据集包含丰富的部分级注释，能够将矢量草图分割成语义部分。实验结果表明，结合结构化的部分级数据和视觉反馈，可以实现可解释、可控且局部可编辑的文本到矢量草图生成。
